description:
Goal: rework compiler2's source-consuming job/state model so indexing, scoping, module definition, contract derivation, body lowering, guard dispatch, entry dispatch, and keying operate from Fz `AnyValueRef` AST roots plus stable source identity instead of old `Rc<Item>` / `FnDef` authority.

Grounding:
- `CodeState::Indexed` currently stores `Vec<Rc<Item>>` and uses old AST pointer/content shape for source identity.
- `ModuleSourceKind::Body` and module source records currently expose old item lists.
- `FunctionDef` currently stores `ast: FnDef`; `DeriveFunctionContract`, `LowerFunction`, `ReifyGuardDispatch`, `PlanEntryDispatch`, and keying all read that old AST shape through `world.function_definition`.
- That shape cannot be the compiler2 source authority once parser/macro expansion produce Fz-shaped AST roots.

Signal:
- `IndexCode` records rooted Fz source values and source identities, then publishes `CodeIndexed` without storing `Rc<Item>` as canonical compiler2 source.
- `ScopeCode` and `DefineModule` discover modules/imports/aliases/protocol forms through compiler2 AST readers over `AnyValueRef` roots.
- Function/module/type/protocol definition state stores source-root identity sufficient to wake dependent facts when macro-expanded source changes.
- Contract/body/dispatch/keying jobs consume function source roots through typed Fz AST cursors/readers; they do not require `FunctionDef.ast: FnDef` as authority.
- Tests prove a source-root identity change republishes `FunctionDefined` and wakes lower/contract/dispatch jobs even when old Rust pointer identity would have been stable.
- Tests prove rebuilding equal quoted source into different heap addresses does not by itself republish semantic facts.
- No compiler2 job calls the old frontend macro/desugar path or performs a repeated Fz-value -> Rust AST -> Fz-value loop.

Change:
- introduce compiler2-owned source-root state/newtypes around `AnyValueRef` for code, module source, and function source, with explicit rooting/GC ownership in the compile-time process and explicit stable source identity/fingerprint fields.
- replace `CodeState::Indexed { items, attrs }` with a rooted source representation plus narrow reader APIs for top-level forms and attributes.
- replace `FunctionDef.ast: FnDef` authority with function source-root state; keep only non-source metadata (`code`, owner module, namespace, capture params) as ordinary Rust fields.
- port contract/body/dispatch/keying walkers from old AST structs to compiler2 Fz AST readers/cursors.
- make revision comparisons use source identity/fingerprint and relevant non-source metadata, never `Rc::ptr_eq` and never raw heap-local `AnyValueRef` addresses.
- if a downstream phase genuinely needs a compact Rust record, derive it once as an internal artifact/fact from the source root; it must not become a second canonical AST or a macro-expansion transport format.
- update telemetry to report raw source-root/function/code ids, source identities, and revisions so tests observe the source-root path.

Why:
- Fz macros return `AnyValueRef` AST roots. If compiler2 jobs still require old Rust AST structs, the architecture silently reintroduces the conversion loop we are explicitly removing.
- Correct-by-construction source production means all source-consuming jobs read the same rooted Fz value graph that parser, macros, and `Fz.Compiler` services produce, while fact revision remains stable across allocation accidents.
title: Compiler2 job model: consume source AST roots as AnyValueRef
